content:
# elizaos/elizaos.github.io Weekly Report (Mar 22 - 28, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
This week focused on essential infrastructure maintenance and ongoing strategic discussions regarding the integration of AI-driven commerce and automated workflows. The primary technical update involved a version bump for the project's core framework to ensure stability and performance. Meanwhile, community engagement centered on the intersection of AI agent payments and marketplace ecosystems, signaling a continued interest in the project's Web3 and decentralized intelligence capabilities.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Infrastructure & Dependency Management
Efforts were made to keep the project's underlying framework current and secure.
*   **Next.js Framework Update:** A pull request was initiated to upgrade the `next` dependency from version 16.1.5 to 16.2.1. This update, managed within the minor-and-patch group, ensures the documentation site benefits from the latest stability fixes and features provided by the framework ([#245](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/pull/245)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### New & Active Issues
Discussions this week were dominated by the practical application of AI agents in autonomous environments, specifically focusing on code quality and financial transactions.
*   **AI Code Review Integration:** Ongoing dialogue persists regarding the implementation of automated AI code reviews. Recent contributions to this discussion suggest a growing interest in utilizing AI-to-AI marketplaces to facilitate these technical workflows ([#4893](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/issues/4893)).
*   **AI Agent Payment Implementation:** The community continues to refine the "AI Agent Payment Implementation Guide." This remains a critical area of focus as the project seeks to define how agents interact with payment protocols and decentralized marketplaces ([#6244](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/issues/6244)).

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The week saw active participation from community members interested in the commercial viability of AI agents. Specifically, there was notable engagement from contributors highlighting the synergy between ElizaOS and external AI-to-AI marketplaces like MAXIA. This interaction underscores a trend toward viewing ElizaOS agents not just as isolated tools, but as participants in a broader, distributed economic ecosystem. The collaboration remains focused on bridging the gap between agent autonomy and practical Web3 payment solutions.
